general_rules.description: LDS stride=32 causes bank conflicts: 353 GB/s vs 878 GB/s at stride=22 (60% penalty)
general_rules.recommendation: Use stride=22 or other non-power-of-2 strides to avoid 32-bank LDS conflicts

general_rules.description: Stride-1 (coalesced) memory access provides 2-10x higher bandwidth than strided access
general_rules.recommendation: Ensure global memory accesses are coalesced (consecutive threads access consecutive addresses)

general_rules.description: Coalesced access achieves 2562 GB/s (2.7x DRAM peak) via 96MB Infinity Cache
general_rules.recommendation: Keep working sets under 96MB and use stride-1 access to benefit from Infinity Cache's ~2.5x bandwidth amplification

general_rules.description: Block size 128 provides best bandwidth (839 GB/s)
general_rules.recommendation: Use block_size=128 (4 waves of 32) for memory-bound kernels on RDNA3

general_rules.description: Use 7 independent FMA accumulator chains for 50.1 TFLOPS vs 21.5 TFLOPS with serial chain (2.3x speedup)
general_rules.recommendation: RDNA3 FMA latency is ~4 cycles. Use 7 independent accumulator chains at block_size=512 to saturate the FMA pipeline

general_rules.description: Use 11 independent half2 FMA chains for 57.9 FP16 TFLOPS vs 55.5 TFLOPS with serial chain (1.0x speedup)
general_rules.recommendation: RDNA3 dual-issue FP16 via packed half2. Use 11 independent half2 chains at block_size=1024 to saturate both FP16 pipelines

general_rules.description: WMMA achieves 88.8 TFLOPS with 6 chains, k_tiles=34 (16x16x16 per instruction)
general_rules.recommendation: Use WMMA with 6 independent accumulator chains and k_tiles>=34 for peak matrix throughput

general_rules.description: Atomic throughput: 431 GOPS uncontended vs 2.9 GOPS fully contended (99% penalty)
general_rules.recommendation: Spread atomic targets across unique addresses. Use shared memory atomics + final global reduce for reductions

general_rules.description: Memory bandwidth saturates at compute_intensity=2 (781 GB/s). Beyond this, kernel is compute-bound
general_rules.recommendation: For memory-bound kernels, add at least 2 FMAs per loaded element to hide memory latency
